name: claude-cli-safe-mode-no-tools-flags
tags: claude cli flags safe-mode tools subprocess subagent bareclaudeagent haiku
body:
The `claude` CLI supports `--safe-mode` (disables customizations/settings discovery) and `--tools ""` (empty string disables all tools; "default" enables all) as real, confirmed flags — verified via `claude --help` on 2026-07-04. Used in langchain_learning/subagent.py's BareClaudeAgent (safe_mode/no_tools constructor kwargs) for the task-context summarizer subprocess, so it runs as a pure text-in/text-out compressor with no tool access.

Why: needed a locked-down subprocess invocation of Claude for context summarization (task:b9dded3e) without inventing flags.

How to apply: reach for these flags whenever spawning a `claude -p` subprocess that should never read/write files or use tools — cheaper and more explicit than an empty settings.json alone (subagent.py already writes one for hook suppression, safe-mode/tools flags are the belt-and-suspenders addition).
